title: Day 10 — E-Commerce Product Listing
summary: Filter, sort, and search product lists with array chaining, type product models, render responsive grids with stable keys, and introduce React.memo for list performance.

blocks.content: Day 09 optimized server search. Day 10 optimizes list UI. Every e-commerce browse page — Amazon, Flipkart, Myntra — filters, sorts, and renders hundreds of products. You will chain array methods to derive a filtered list, render it in a responsive grid, and prevent unnecessary re-renders with React.memo.

blocks.title: Week 2 Day 3
blocks.content: You know useState, map, and keys from Week 1. Today adds filter/sort pipelines and React.memo. Do NOT introduce Context API (Day 12) or Redux (Week 3).
filter returns a new array matching a predicate. Never mutate the source array. Chain multiple filters for category, price range, and search term.

blocks.code:
const filtered = products
  .filter((p) => category === 'all' || p.category === category)
  .filter((p) => p.price >= minPrice && p.price <= maxPrice)
  .filter((p) => p.name.toLowerCase().includes(search.toLowerCase()));

blocks.content: sort mutates in place — always copy first with spread or slice. Return negative, zero, or positive from compare function for ascending order.

blocks.title: useMemo for derived lists
blocks.content: Wrap filter + sort in useMemo([products, category, minPrice, maxPrice, search, sortBy]) so you only recompute when inputs change — not on every render.

blocks.title: Never mutate state arrays
blocks.content: products.sort() inside render mutates state directly — a React anti-pattern. Always create a new array. This prepares you for immutable updates on Day 12.
Define a Product interface matching your data source. Use union types for category enums and optional fields for sale prices or ratings.

blocks.title: Separate filter state types
blocks.content: Define FilterState or individual useState hooks for category, priceMin, priceMax, search, sortBy. Typed state prevents impossible filter combinations at compile time.
Map filtered products to ProductCard components. Each list item needs a key — React uses keys to match items across re-renders. Wrong keys cause bugs when lists reorder.

rows: Changes when list is sorted or filtered
New key every render — full remount

blocks.text: React.memo — skip re-render when props unchanged

blocks.content: When parent filter state changes, only cards with changed props should re-render. React.memo wraps ProductCard and shallow-compares props. If product object reference is unchanged, skip render.
Large lists where parent re-renders often but individual item props stay the same. Product grids, comment threads, data tables.
Small lists, props that always change (inline objects/functions), or components that always re-render anyway. Profile before memoizing everything.

blocks.title: Hints
blocks.content: Fake Store API needs no key: https://fakestoreapi.com/products. Use CSS Grid with auto-fill and minmax for responsive columns. Verify memo with React DevTools 'Highlight updates'.
Symptom: Inputs lose focus or wrong product shows after sort. Cause: index as key. Fix: product.id — stable across reorder.
Symptom: All cards re-render on filter change. Cause: Inline onClick={() => ...} or new object props every render defeats memo. Fix: memo + stable props; useCallback only when profiling shows need.
Symptom: Products disappear unexpectedly. Cause: Mutating products with .sort(), or off-by-one price comparisons. Fix: Copy before sort; test boundary values.
